wardrobe.default: The AITX open-arms mark brought to life: a round head with a joyful closed-eye smile and a wide open grin, two arrowhead arms raised up and out in a celebratory welcome, and a lower body that IS the AITX split mark (two mirrored orange arrowhead legs with a clean vertical split straight down the middle). Solid AITX orange #ff4201. A small heart often floats above the head.

structured.promptCraft: Joy is the AITX logo as an animated character, an 'animated logo', not a separate-species mascot. HARD RULE: the lower body / legs are the AITX mark's TWO rigid geometric arrowhead shapes with a vertical split down the middle — they are LOGO shapes, NEVER anatomical legs (no knees, feet, or bending). Dynamic walking/jumping poses collapse this into human legs and MUST be avoided; keep Joy in static or turning poses. The character read comes only from the joyful face and the raised welcoming arms. In busy scenes, pass MULTIPLE angle sheets (front + a three-quarter) so the split holds. Distinct from Chip (the brand agent and czar): Chip does the making, Joy is the animated face of the mark. Whether Joy becomes the official mascot is an open brand question (see the keynote).
